title: helianthemum italicum var. oelandicum
intro: Helianthemum italicum var. oelandicum is a rare and endemic subspecies of rockrose, a flowering plant native to the island of Öland in Sweden. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. The plant can be used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
